name: Eastern Front (1941)
description:
Eastern Front (1941) is a turn-based strategy game focusing on the initial stages of Operation Barbarossa, the German invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941. The player can choose to play as either the German Wehrmacht or the Soviet Red Army. The game map encompasses the vast territory from the Baltic Sea to the Black Sea, divided into hex-based regions representing terrain like forests, marshes, plains, and cities. Each side controls a variety of military units, including infantry divisions, armored divisions, motorized divisions, artillery units, and air support. Units have attributes such as strength, movement range, attack, and defense, influenced by factors like weather, terrain, and supply. 

The German player aims to achieve a swift victory by capturing key Soviet cities like Moscow, Leningrad, and Kiev, while inflicting heavy casualties on the Red Army. The Soviet player must hold out against the initial onslaught, inflicting attritional losses, and buying time to mobilize reserves and launch counter-offensives. Victory conditions are determined by a combination of capturing objectives, inflicting casualties, and controlling key strategic regions. The game will feature a dynamic weather system that impacts unit movement and combat effectiveness. Supply lines will be a crucial element, requiring players to secure and maintain logistical routes. The game will also include events that reflect historical occurrences during this period, adding an element of unpredictability. The game has a grand campaign mode, plus several historical scenario modes that allow for shorter playtime with specific objectives and units.

controls.arrow_left: Scroll the map to the left.
controls.arrow_right: Scroll the map to the right.
controls.w: Select the next available unit within the current view.
controls.a: Cycle through available units in the currently selected hex.
controls.s: Call up/dismiss the unit details panel for the currently selected unit or hex.
controls.space: Center the map on the currently selected unit.
controls.shift: Hold to display unit movement range on the map.
controls.z: Initiate an attack with the selected unit on the target hex.
controls.x: Fortify the selected unit’s position in its current hex (increases defensive bonus).
controls.c: Cancel any current unit action or order.
